topics: 👔 ערכין and the carve-out for clothing of wife and children
💰 קרן + חומש + אשם — the three-part payment for false oath
🤝 Deposits from women, slaves, and minors — and what to do at death
💍 R' Abba bar Chana's wife: 'אין אדם מצוי לשקר בשעת מיתה'

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Reaching the Victim and Returning the Object
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: Daf 40 closes the ערכין discussion (R' Hoshaya teaches that foreclosure on movables for valuations is rabbinic, not biblical) and then opens Mishnah 9:6: the robber who took something worth a peruṭa and swore falsely must pursue the victim even to Media. The Yerushalmi works out the chain of payments (קרן, חומש, אשם), the rules for who counts as a valid pickup-agent, and the case of a deposit confessed at the moment of death — culminating in Rav's ruling on R' Abba bar Chana's wife: אֵין אָדָם מָצוּי לְשַׁקֵּר בִּשְׁעַת מִיתָה.
overview.hebrewSource: הַגּוֹזֵל אֶת חֲבֵירוֹ שָׁוֶה פְרוּטָה וְנִשְׁבַּע לוֹ יוֹלִיכֶנּוּ אַחֲרָיו אֲפִילוּ לְמָדַי

slides.title: 🏷️ Closing the ערכין Sugya — Movables Are Only Rabbinic
slides.label: R' Hoshaya in the name of Shmuel bar Abba:
slides.text: The Mishnah excludes the wife and children's clothing from valuation foreclosure. The Yerushalmi probes: what if someone said 'my valuation on me on condition that you not foreclose on my wife's and children's clothing'? R' Nasa: he can stipulate against any specific item. R' Hoshaya draws the inference: זֹאת אוֹמֶרֶת שֶׁאֵין שָׁמִין עֲרָכִין מִן הַמִּטַּלְטְלַין דְּבַר תּוֹרָה אֶלָּא מִדִּבְרֵיהֶן — biblically, valuations are foreclosed only from real estate; movables are rabbinic.
slides.hebrewSource: זֹאת אוֹמֶרֶת שֶׁאֵין שָׁמִין עֲרָכִין מִן הַמִּטַּלְטְלַין דְּבַר תּוֹרָה אֶלָּא מִדִּבְרֵיהֶן

slides.title: 🚶 The Robber Who Swore Falsely — Pursuit to Media
slides.subtitle: Mishnah 9:6 and the chain of consequences.

steps.content: 1️⃣ Robber took something worth at least a פְּרוּטָה and swore falsely denying it.
2️⃣ He must personally deliver the object to the victim — אֲפִילוּ לְמָדַי (even to Media).
3️⃣ He may not hand it to the victim's son or agent — only to שְׁלִיחַ בֵּית דִּין.
4️⃣ If the victim has died, the robber repays the heirs.
5️⃣ He pays קרן + חומש + אשם; if the principal drops below a peruṭa via partial payment, the chase ends.

callout.label: Why so strict:
callout.text: The false oath converted a civil debt into a personal religious obligation. The robber must literally place the object back in the victim's hand.

slides.title: 💸 The חומש-on-חומש Cascade
slides.subtitle: What happens when the robber swears again on the fifth.
rows: 2. Pays principal but swears falsely on the fifth
הֲרֵי זֶה מְשַׁלֵּם חוֹמֶשׁ עַל חוֹמֶשׁ
New chumash on the unpaid chumash
Each unpaid fifth becomes a fresh principal
עַד שֶׁיִּתְמָעֵט חוֹמֶשׁ הָאַחֲרוֹן פָּחוֹת מִשָּׁוֶה פְרוּטָה

callout.label: R' Yonatan's principle:
callout.text: Each unpaid חומש legally morphs into a new קרן the moment the robber swears falsely on it. The fifth piggybacks on its own fifth, recursively, until the residue dips below a peruṭa.

slides.title: 🤝 Deposits From Women, Slaves, and Minors
slides.subtitle: The Tannaitic rule on accepting deposits from those without independent legal standing.

boxes.title: 👩 From a woman
items: If she died: return to her husband

boxes.title: 🧑 From a slave
items: If he died: return to his master

boxes.title: 👶 From a minor
items: If he died: return to his father as סְגוּלָּה (safe investment)

boxes.title: 💬 Deathbed declaration
items: If the depositor named someone at death: יַעֲשֶׂה פֵירוּשׁ לְפֵירוּשׁוֹ

callout.label: Logic of the defaults:
callout.text: The presumption is that whatever each dependent class held actually belongs to the responsible party (husband, master, father). A deathbed naming overrides the default — but only if witnessed.

slides.title: 💍 The Story: R' Abba bar Chana's Wife
slides.subtitle: Rav's ruling on dying-words testimony.

nodes.content: R' Abba bar Chana's wife is dying

She declares: אָהֵן קִידּוּשָׁא דִבְרַתִּי
(these rings belong to my daughter)

columns.label: Case before Rav
columns.content: Rav rules for the wife's words

nodes.content: 🎓 אֵין אָדָם מָצוּי לְשַׁקֵּר בִּשְׁעַת מִיתָה — people do not lie at death.

callout.label: Why this matters:
callout.text: Rav's principle becomes a foundational rule for halakhic deathbed testimony: a person at the threshold of death is presumed credible. The case-law is built right into the daf.

summary.principle: A false oath transforms civil restitution into personal pursuit — the robber must reach the victim even to Media, paying קרן+חומש+אשם, with each unpaid fifth recursively becoming new principal. The daf closes with the parallel honesty-presumption: a person at death is not assumed to lie.

flowSteps.content: End of ערכין: movables are foreclosed only מדבריהן
Each sugya tracks who can speak for whom: the robber pursuing the victim, the depositor naming an heir, the wife claiming the rings.

🎓 Key Principle:
Halakhah handles representation carefully — false oaths force personal pursuit, and a deathbed statement carries presumptive truth.

quiz.q: What inference does Rav Hoshaya draw from the Mishnah's allowance to stipulate against foreclosing on a particular item?
o: Valuations are not Torah law at all
Foreclosure of valuations from movables is rabbinic, not biblical
Movables are never foreclosed
Only women's clothing is exempt
quiz.e: זֹאת אוֹמֶרֶת שֶׁאֵין שָׁמִין עֲרָכִין מִן הַמִּטַּלְטְלַין דְּבַר תּוֹרָה אֶלָּא מִדִּבְרֵיהֶן.

quiz.q: If a robber took at least a peruṭa's worth and swore falsely, where must he deliver the object?
o: Only within his own city
To beit din
אֲפִילוּ לְמָדַי — even to Media (he must follow the victim)
He may give it to the victim's son
quiz.e: The Mishnah: יוֹלִיכֶנּוּ אַחֲרָיו אֲפִילוּ לְמָדַי. He may not give it to the victim's son or agent — only שליח בית דין.

quiz.q: According to R' Yonatan, what happens when the robber swears falsely on the unpaid חומש?
o: Nothing further — he already paid קרן
בִּנְתִינַת הַחוֹמֶשׁ נַעֲשֶׂה חוֹמֶשׁ קֶרֶן — each unpaid fifth becomes a new principal
The case is dismissed
Only an additional אשם is due
quiz.e: R' Yonatan reads the Mishnah's חומש-on-חומש cascade as: each unpaid fifth turns into fresh principal, generating its own fifth — until the residue is below a peruṭa.

quiz.q: If a deposit was accepted from a woman and she dies, to whom is it returned (per the Tannaitic rule cited)?
o: To her father
To her husband
To beit din
To her oldest son
quiz.e: מֵתָה יַחֲזִיר לְבַעֲלָהּ. The default is that her belongings revert to her husband's domain. A deathbed naming (with witnesses) can override this.

quiz.q: What did Rav rule in the case of R' Abba bar Chana's dying wife claiming the rings belonged to her daughter?
o: The husband keeps the rings
The case is split between husband and daughter
The wife's words stand: אֵין אָדָם מָצוּי לְשַׁקֵּר בִּשְׁעַת מִיתָה
The rings go to beit din until further proof
quiz.e: Rav established the principle that a person at the moment of death is presumed truthful. The rings go to the daughter as the wife declared.
